page: Microsoft Azure D8a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, alongside 205 GB of local storage. No GPU accelerators are included in this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate overall performance. The server registers average results across most standard workloads, including Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU and memory metrics, and Redis SET operations. However, stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and multi-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance for both small and large blocks, though large-block write bandwidth is weaker, falling into the bottom 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform at average levels, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression shows weaker throughput.

Given its hardware profile, the D8a_v4 offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard business applications. The inclusion of 205 GB of local storage provides built-in space for temporary files or caching without requiring external attachments. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and lightweight text or image processing. It is less optimal for compute-intensive tasks or high-throughput multi-threaded processing due to its lower relative CPU benchmark scores.
description: Microsoft Azure D8a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7763 processor featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores), a 3.525 GHz clock speed, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system includes 32.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most tasks, including Redis operations, static web serving, and Geekbench suites, though it exhibits weaker results in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory writes. Qualitatively, the D8a_v4 provides a balanced resource profile for standard workloads. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation, while being less optimal for highly intensive compute or multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 32 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, suitable for standard database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8a_v4 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 32 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, moderate memory capacity, and integrated local storage for standard workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 32 GB of system memory and 205 GB of local storage
Delivers average performance for Redis, web serving, and database operations
Shows lower relative performance in intensive single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks
Best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and web hosting
